title: agrostis stolonifera var. hispida
intro: Agrostis stolonifera var. hispida, commonly known as Creeping Bentgrass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Creeping Bentgrass are attributed to its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and aid in digestion. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as be used in the form of tinctures or extracts, allowing for various methods of consumption and application.
